segments.text: JOHN: Erm... hello Professor, I'm John Wishart.
segments.translation: 约翰：呃…… 教授，您好！我是约翰·威沙特。

segments.text: I'm working on my entry for the Global Design Competition.
segments.translation: 我正在为参加全球设计大赛努力。

segments.text: My tutor said you might be able to help me with it.
segments.translation: 我的导师告诉我您能帮助我。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: Ah, yes, I got a copy of your drawings.
segments.translation: 教授：啊，是的，我拿到了一份你的绘画。

segments.text: Come in and tell me about it.
segments.translation: 快进来，跟我具体说说。

segments.text: What sort of competition is it?
segments.translation: 这是什么类型的比赛？

segments.text: JOHN: Well, it's an international design competition and we have to come up with a new design for a typical domestic kitchen appliance.
segments.translation: 约翰：好吧，这是一个国际设计大赛，我们必须根据常见的家用厨房电器想出一个新的设计。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: I see, and are there any special conditions? Does it have to save energy for example?
segments.translation: 教授：我明白了。有什么特别的条件吗？比如需要节能吗？

segments.text: JOHN: Actually that was the focus in last year's competition.
segments.translation: 约翰：实际上，这是去年大赛的重点。

segments.text: This year's different.
segments.translation: 今年的不一样。

segments.text: We have to adopt an innovative approach to existing technology, using it in a way that hasn't been thought of before.
segments.translation: 我们需要提出对现有技术的创新性、突破性使用方法。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: I see, that sounds tricky.
segments.translation: 教授：我明白了。这听起来有点棘手。

segments.text: And what kitchen appliance have you chosen?
segments.translation: 你选择了哪种厨房电器？

segments.text: JOHN: Well, I decided to choose the dishwasher.
segments.translation: 约翰：嗯，我选择了洗碗机。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: Interesting, what made you choose that?
segments.translation: 教授：有意思。是什么原因让你选择了它？

segments.text: JOHN: Well, they're an everyday kitchen appliance in most Australian houses but they're all pretty boring and almost identical to each other.
segments.translation: 约翰：在大部分澳洲的住房里，这是一件必备的厨房电器，但是它们都很乏味，看起来也基本都一样。

segments.text: I think some people will be prepared to pay a little extra for something that looks different.
segments.translation: 我想人们应该可以接受多花点钱，得到一个不太一样的东西。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: That's a nice idea.
segments.translation: 教授：这个想法不错。

segments.text: I see you've called your design 'the Rockpool'; why is that?
segments.translation: 我看到你把你的设计命名为“石潭”，为什么呢？

segments.text: JOHN: Basically because it looks like the rock pools you find on a beach.
segments.translation: 约翰：基本上因为它看起来像是海滩上的潮水潭。

segments.text: The top is made of glass so that you can look down into it.
segments.translation: 它的顶部是玻璃制成的，所以你可以看到里面。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: And there's a stone at the bottom.
segments.translation: 教授：底部还有石头。

segments.text: Is that just for decoration?
segments.translation: 那个只是装饰吗？

segments.text: JOHN: Actually it does have a function.
segments.translation: 约翰：实际上它是有作用的。

segments.text: Instead of pushing a button, you turn the stone.
segments.translation: 我们不按按钮，取而代之的是转动这块石头。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: So it's really just a novel way of starting the dishwasher.
segments.translation: 教授：所以这真是启动洗碗机的一个创新的办法。

segments.text: JOHN: That's right.
segments.translation: 约翰：是的。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: It's a really nice design, but what makes it innovative?
segments.translation: 教授：这真是个不错的设计。但是有什么使它具有创新性吗？

segments.text: JOHN: Well, I decided to make a dishwasher that uses carbon dioxide.
segments.translation: 约翰：嗯，我决定制作一个使用二氧化碳的洗碗机。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: In place of water and detergent?
segments.translation: 教授：代替水和洗洁精？

segments.text: How will you manage that?
segments.translation: 怎么能做到呢？

segments.text: JOHN: The idea is to pressurise the carbon dioxide so that it becomes a liquid.
segments.translation: 约翰：主要的想法是给二氧化碳加压，这样它就会变为液体。

segments.text: The fluid is then released into the dishwasher where it cleans the dishes all by itself.
segments.translation: 之后这种液体会被释放到洗碗机中，它会自己清洁盘子。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: Sounds like a brilliant idea!
segments.translation: 教授：这听起来真是个很有才华的想法。

segments.text: Your system will totally do away with the need for strong detergents.
segments.translation: 你的系统完全省掉了强效洗洁精。

segments.text: So what happens once the dishes are clean?
segments.translation: 那么当盘子洗干净后会发生什么？

segments.text: JOHN: Well, to allow them to dry, the liquid carbon dioxide and the waste materials all go to an area called the holding chamber.
segments.translation: 约翰：好的，为了让它们干燥，液体的二氧化碳和废物会去到一个叫做容留室的地方。

segments.text: That's where the liquid is depressurised and so it reverts to a gas.
segments.translation: 在这里对液体进行减压，所以它会变回气体。

segments.text: Then the oil and grease are separated out and sent to the waste system.
segments.translation: 之后油污会被分离出来进入废物系统。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: It sounds like you've thought it all out very thoroughly.
segments.translation: 教授：听起来你已经全都彻底想清楚了。

segments.text: So, what happens to the carbon dioxide once the process is complete? Not wasted I hope.
segments.translation: 所以，一旦整个程序完成后，二氧化碳气体会怎么样？我希望不是浪费掉了。

segments.text: JOHN: Actually, that's where the real savings are made.
segments.translation: 约翰：实际上，这才是真正节约的地方。

segments.text: The carbon dioxide is sent back to the cylinder and can be used again and again.
segments.translation: 二氧化碳会被送回气缸，可以不断重复使用。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: What a terrific idea.
segments.translation: 教授：多棒的主意啊！

segments.text: Do you think it will ever be built?
segments.translation: 你认为会有人生产这个吗？

segments.text: JOHN: Probably not, but that's OK.
segments.translation: 约翰：也许不会，但是没关系。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: Well, I'm sure a lot of positive things will come out of your design.
segments.translation: 教授：好吧，我肯定一定会有很多积极的东西从你的设计中迸发出来。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: Now, you seem to have thought about everything so what exactly did you need me to help you with?
segments.translation: 教授：现在，你似乎已经考虑好了所有的东西，那么你到底需要我帮你做什么呢？

segments.text: JOHN: Well, my design has made it to the final stage of the competition and, in a few months' time, I have to give a presentation, and that's the part I was hoping you could held me with.
segments.translation: 约翰：呃，我的设计已经到了竞赛的最后的最后一个环节，几个月后，我必须做一个演讲，这就是我希望你可以帮助我的地方。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: Right, well that should be easy enough.
segments.translation: 教授：好的，这应该很容易。

segments.text: What have you managed to do so far?
segments.translation: 你现在已经做了什么？

segments.text: JOHN: Well, I've got detailed drawings to show how it will work and I've also written a 500-word paper on it.
segments.translation: 约翰：嗯，我已经完成了细节的图画，用以展示它如何工作，我还写了一个关于它的500字的稿子。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: I see. Well, if you want to stand a good chance of winning you really need a model of the machine.
segments.translation: 教授：我明白了。如果你想有赢得比赛的希望，你需要一个这个机器的模型。

segments.text: JOHN: Yes, I thought I might but I'm having a few problems.
segments.translation: 约翰：是的，我想我可能会做一个，但是我遇到了一些问题。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: What is the main difficulty so far?
segments.translation: 教授：目前最大的困难是什么？

segments.text: Let me guess - is it the materials?
segments.translation: 让我猜猜——是材料吗？

segments.text: JOHN: Yes. I want it to look professional but everything that's top quality is also very expensive.
segments.translation: 约翰：是的。我想看起来专业些，但是所有高质量的材料都很贵。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: Look, projects like this are very important to us.
segments.translation: 教授：注意，像这样的项目对于我们来说很重要。

segments.text: They really help lift our profile.
segments.translation: 它可以帮助我们提升我们的形象。

segments.text: So why don't you talk to the university about a grant?
segments.translation: 所以你为什么不和学校谈谈助学金的事呢？

segments.text: I can help you fill out the application forms if you like.
segments.translation: 如果你愿意，我可以帮你填申请表。

segments.text: JOHN: That would be great.
segments.translation: 约翰：那太好了。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: You'd better show me this paper you've written as well.
segments.translation: 教授：你最好也给我看看你写的稿子。

segments.text: For a global competition such as this you need to make sure the technical details you've given are accurate and thorough.
segments.translation: 对于一个像这样的国际比赛，你需要确保你给出的每一个技术细节都是准确和清晰的。

segments.text: JOHN: That would be a great help.
segments.translation: 约翰：那对我真是莫大的帮助。

segments.text: PROFESSOR: Is there anything else I can do?
segments.translation: 教授：还有其他我可以做的吗？

segments.text: JOHN: Well, I'm really ...
segments.translation: 约翰：呃，我真的……
